subject: math
content: We count to know how many things there are. We start at one and go up. Counting helps us every day.

subject: math
content: You can count to 10 on your fingers. After 10 comes 11, then 12. Numbers keep going up and up.

subject: math
content: Adding means putting numbers together. Two apples plus three apples equals five apples. The plus sign means we are adding.

subject: math
content: When we add zero to a number it stays the same. Five plus zero equals five. Zero means nothing is added.

subject: math
content: Subtracting means taking away. If you have five cookies and eat two, you have three left. The minus sign means we are subtracting.

subject: math
content: A circle is round with no corners. A square has four equal sides and four corners. A triangle has three sides and three corners.

subject: math
content: A rectangle has four sides. Two sides are longer and two sides are shorter. A door is shaped like a rectangle.

subject: math
content: A pattern repeats in the same order. Red, blue, red, blue is a pattern. Patterns are everywhere in the world around us.

subject: math
content: Numbers can make patterns too. 2, 4, 6, 8 is a pattern. Each number goes up by two.

subject: math
content: We measure things to find out how long or tall they are. We can use a ruler to measure. Inches and centimeters are units of measurement.

subject: math
content: We can compare sizes. This book is bigger than that one. This pencil is shorter than that one. Comparing helps us understand size.

subject: math
content: Numbers have names. The number 1 is called one. The number 10 is called ten. Each number has its own special name.

subject: math
content: Even numbers can be split into two equal groups. Two, four, six, and eight are even numbers. Odd numbers cannot be split equally.

subject: math
content: We use clocks to tell time. There are 60 minutes in one hour. There are 24 hours in one day.

subject: math
content: Sorting means putting things into groups. We can sort by color, shape, or size. Sorting helps us organize things.

subject: math
content: We can compare numbers. The greater than sign is > and the less than sign is <. Bigger numbers are greater.

subject: math
content: Numbers have tens and ones. In 23, the 2 means two tens. The 3 means three ones.

subject: math
content: A fraction is a part of a whole. Half means two equal parts. If you split a cookie in half, you get two pieces.

subject: math
content: Money has coins. A penny is worth 1 cent. A dime is worth 10 cents.

subject: math
content: Temperature tells how hot or cold it is. We read it with a thermometer. Hot is warmer, and cold is cooler.
